channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Warriors goalie Chipezeze sticks with relegated Magesi FC
item.description: ZIMBABWE senior men’s national soccer team Warriors goalkeeper Elvis Chipezeze will not be leaving Magesi FC, even after the club’s relegation from the Betway Premiership. The Limpopo club slipped out of South Africa’s top flight at the end of the 2025/26 season, sparking speculation that the 40-year-old would seek another top-tier team. But new Magesi [...] The post Warriors goalie Chipezeze sticks with relegated Magesi FC appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Rights groups condemn alleged intimidation of community activists after Parliament testimony on Chinese mine
item.description: FOUR Zimbabwean civil society organisations have accused a senior government official of intimidating community activists after they gave evidence to Parliament about alleged environmental and labour abuses linked to a Chinese-owned mining company in Buhera district. In a joint statement issued Friday, the Centre for Natural Resource Governance (CNRG), Zimbabwe Diamond and Allied Workers Union [...] The post Rights groups condemn alleged intimidation of community activists after Parliament testimony on Chinese mine appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: The threat to democracy: Lessons from Zimbabwe for South Africa
item.description: By Tswelopele Makoe THIS past Tuesday, Zimbabwe’s 83-year-old President Emmerson Mnangagwa scandalously imposed a new law that irretrievably tightens his grip on power. In a deeply controversial move, he signed legislation extending his stay in the Presidency by two years beyond the end of his term. More alarmingly, the legislation effectively strips Zimbabweans of their [...] The post The threat to democracy: Lessons from Zimbabwe for South Africa appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Teachers’ Union mobilizes against Mnangagwa’s term extension, plans parliament protest
item.description: THE Amalgamated Rural Teachers Union of Zimbabwe (ARTUZ) has resolved to join a broader alliance of civic groups to resist Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3, warning that the changes threaten democratic protections. At a National Executive Council (NEC) meeting held Saturday, ARTUZ adopted a raft of resolutions aimed at defending constitutional rights, improving teachers’ welfare, [...] The post Teachers’ Union mobilizes against Mnangagwa’s term extension, plans parliament protest appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Teachers Union mobilises against President Mnangagwa term extension, sets protest agenda over poor salaries
item.description: THE Amalgamated Rural Teachers Union of Zimbabwe (ARTUZ) has resolved to join a broader alliance of civic groups to resist Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3, warning that the changes threaten democratic protections. At a National Executive Council (NEC) meeting held Saturday, ARTUZ adopted a raft of resolutions aimed at defending constitutional rights, improving teachers’ welfare, [...] The post Teachers Union mobilises against President Mnangagwa term extension, sets protest agenda over poor salaries appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Macheso pulls out of Silala Festival over South Africa unrest @bulawayo24
item.description: Sungura legend Alick Macheso has cancelled his scheduled appearance at this year’s Silala Festival in South Africa, citing security concerns arising from ongoing protests and unrest affecting foreign... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Coach Rambo involved in accident @bulawayo24
item.description: Renowned Zimbabwean rescue diver Victor Kazembe, popularly known as Coach Rambo, was involved in an accident near Norton on Saturday night, Bulawayo24 has learnt.Witness Tawanda Honourable Chisasa tol... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Egypt Denies Entry to LGBTQ+ Cruise Ship Days After Turkey Also Turned It Away
item.description: CAIRO, Egypt — Egyptian authorities have refused to let a cruise ship carrying roughly 2,000 mostly LGBTQ+ passengers dock at Alexandria, just days after Turkey blocked the same vessel from two of its ports. Passengers aboard Virgin Voyages’ Scarlet Lady, chartered by the U.S.-based LGBTQ+ travel company Atlantis Events for a 10-day sailing from Athens [...] The post Egypt Denies Entry to LGBTQ+ Cruise Ship Days After Turkey Also Turned It Away appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Trump and Iran’s supreme leader trade threats as mediators try to save their crumbling deal
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates — U.S. and Iranian leaders traded threats on Saturday as the interim deal to end the war buckled under crossfire in the Middle East and efforts continued to keep talks going. President Donald Trump overnight made threats on social media of further missile attacks against Iran, after the funeral of Supreme [...] The post Trump and Iran’s supreme leader trade threats as mediators try to save their crumbling deal appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25 @GaytonMcK
item.description: JOHANNESBURG — South Africa midfielder Jayden Adams, who played at the World Cup, has died. He was 25. His death was confirmed by Gayton McKenzie, South Africa’s minister of sport, arts and culture, in a statement on Saturday. “It is with profound shock and a heavy heart that I have learnt of the passing of [...] The post South Africa World Cup midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25 appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: The Shadow City: How Informality Is Rebuilding Zimbabwe’s Economy from the Ground Up
item.description: New research presented by Injecta Analytics at the Real Estate Institute of Zimbabwe’s Winter School in Kigali makes a striking claim: roughly three in every four operating businesses in the country now sit outside the formal economy. That figure alone would be notable. What makes it consequential is what it implies for anyone trying to [...] The post The Shadow City: How Informality Is Rebuilding Zimbabwe’s Economy from the Ground Up appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Police Close In On Douglas Mwonzora Over Alleged Forgery Investigation
item.description: Former Senator Reportedly Being Sought For Police Interviews As Forgery Probe Reaches Advanced Stage By A Correspondent | The MDC... Continue reading "Police Close In On Douglas Mwonzora Over Alleged Forgery Investigation" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Canal+ Completes US$3 Billion Acquisition of MultiChoice, Creating One of Africa’s Largest Media Groups
item.description: JOHANNESBURG – French media and entertainment group Canal+ has completed its US$3 billion (R55 billion) acquisition of MultiChoice Group, marking the end of a multi-year takeover process that brings Africa’s largest pay-television operator fully under its ownership. The transaction transforms MultiChoice, the parent company of DStv, GOtv and streaming platform Showmax, into a wholly owned [...] The post Canal+ Completes US$3 Billion Acquisition of MultiChoice, Creating One of Africa’s Largest Media Groups appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Mikel Merino stars again as Spain edges Belgium 2-1 in the World Cup quarterfinals
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. — Mikel Merino is Spain’s World Cup hero once again, and even he finds it hard to believe. A mere four days after Merino scored a clutch goal as a substitute in injury time to beat Portugal, he found himself in the right spot again in the quarterfinals when Belgium’s backup goalkeeper spilled [...] The post Mikel Merino stars again as Spain edges Belgium 2-1 in the World Cup quarterfinals appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zim’s Maize Imports Drop 40% as Local Harvests Boost Food Security @263Chat
item.description: By Tinomudaishe Muzanenhamo Zimbabwe’s maize imports fell by more than 40% in the year to May 2026, in a development that industry leaders say reflects improving domestic grain production and growing momentum towards national food security. Figures released by the Zimbabwe National Statistics Agency (ZimStat) [...] The post Zim’s Maize Imports Drop 40% as Local Harvests Boost Food Security appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat
